resposta:
solution: 
```class Solution:
    def minimumSteps(self, s: str) -> int:
        left = 0
        right = len(s) - 1
        steps = 0
        while left < right:
            if s[left] == '1':
                left += 1
            elif s[right] == '0':
                right -= 1
            else:
                while left < right and s[left] == '1':
                    left += 1
                while left < right and s[right] == '0':
                    right -= 1
                steps += 1
        return steps
```

efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(n)
space complexity: O(1)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: This solution uses two pointers, one at the start and one at the end of the string, to track the position of the first '0' and the last '1'. It iterates through the string, swapping adjacent elements when necessary, and keeps track of the number of steps required. The time complexity is O(n) because each element is visited at most twice, and the space complexity is O(1) because only a constant amount of space is used.
